chapter_title.en: Chapter 22 – The reclining and the folded (postures)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/566)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/566)
text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) الِاحْتِبَاءُ حِيطَانُ الْعَرَبِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazan and Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, altogether from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ibrahim Bin Abdul Hameed,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Al Hassan<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘The cross-legged (posture) is an encirclement of the Arabs (sitting in a circle)’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn Isma’il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan and Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father all from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Ibrahim ibn ‘Abd al-Hamid from abu al-Hassan (a.s.) who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah has said, ‘Sitting in the Mosque in al- ‘ Ihtiba ’ posture, (knees folded with thighs against the belly by a piece of cloth or by one’s arms) is the wall of Arabs.’”
